nativeRange.habitat: Hybrid cultivar with no wild source. Parent genus Echinodorus is native to wetlands of the tropical Americas.

decorPreferences: Deep nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil or capped sand) — heavy root feeder
Iron-rich root tabs near the root zone for full ruby-red coloration
Moderate to bright lighting deepens the red coloration
Moderate flow; avoid placement directly in jet returns

summary: A large hybrid Echinodorus cultivar prized for ruby-red coloration through new growth that matures to deep red-green. Classic background sword for medium-to-large planted tanks; standard easy Echinodorus care, with iron supplementation as the key driver of the signature color.
careNotes:
Echinodorus 'Red Rubin' is a HYBRID Echinodorus cultivar — often cited as a Heinz Kasselmann selection from the late 1980s involving Echinodorus barthii × E. horemanii 'Red' parentage, though the exact lineage varies by source. Sold across the trade as Echinodorus sp. 'Red Rubin' or 'Rubin' with no wild population. Adult size is generally 30–60 cm submersed, making it one of the larger common swords. 

Care is the standard Echinodorus profile: a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER that demands a deep nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil, capped sand with root tabs, or laterite). Plant the crown just above the substrate surface — buried crowns rot. Iron-rich root tabs near the root zone are the single biggest driver of the ruby-red coloration that gives this cultivar its name; without iron supplementation, new growth comes in olive-green. Moderate-to-bright lighting deepens the color further. CO2 is not required but injection improves leaf size, density, and color. Temperature 20–28 °C, pH 5.5–7.5, gH 4–18 — same envelope as the rest of the Echinodorus complex. 

Background placement; needs at least 45 cm of vertical clearance and a footprint of 30+ cm. Sister entries: fw-plant-011 (Amazon Sword), fw-plant-127 (Ozelot), fw-plant-128 (Rose), fw-plant-142 (Kleiner Bar), fw-plant-150 (Melon), fw-plant-157 (Marble Queen Radican), fw-plant-158 (Red Flame). 

NOTE: This entry was written from genus/parent baselines after the original research dispatch hung on upstream sources. Reviewer should verify the cultivar parentage and color-response specifics against Tropica or Buce Plant during the manifest URL cleanup pass.

primary.name: Echinodorus 'Rose' parent entry (fw-plant-128) + species pattern inheritance
primary.notes: Genus-baseline inheritance from researched Echinodorus 'Rose' parent. Dispatch attempt hung on upstream retailer sources; manifest URL cleanup pending.
